observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the concerned departments or offices responsible for recording the municipal's road network to collaborate and develop a systematic approach to meet the accounting and reporting requirements stipulated in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that Management reach out to the DILG Regional Office VII to inquire about training schedules for the LRAMS. If such training is available, the concerned officials should be encouraged to attend.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant agreed to reconcile their records and ensure that delinquent RPT and SET as at year-end are recorded in the books of the Municipality under the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts to ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to undertake the following:
Make the necessary adjusting entries, for those with verifiable data or supporting documents, to correct the omissions and errors in the books and prepare timely the BRSs per bank account; and
In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, exert effort to retrieve the supporting documents of the reconciling items without verifiable data, within a specified period, so that proper adjusting entries could be made.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant review all the entries affecting the above-cited accounts and make appropriate adjustments to reflect their correct balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the following courses of action:
The Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed, to ensure the recording of such cancellation.
Municipal Accountant effect the proposed adjusting entry below to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA –
The Municipal Treasurer monitor the outstanding checks if there are any stale checks and send a written notice to the payee of the existence of such check as prescribed in Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the subject reciprocal accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the balances, and conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to adjust or correct any difference.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, agreed to reconcile the accounting records and cash book balances and effect the necessary corrections to consistently harmonize the balances of both records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to expedite the reconciliation of accounts. Meantime and to prevent the accumulation of unreconciled balances, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain subsidiary ledgers and schedules for asset and liability accounts related to current year transactions.

We recommended and the GSO–Designate agreed to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation and updating of the list of insurable properties of the Municipality and thereafter apply with the GSIS property insurance for all covered properties of the Municipality pursuant to RA No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

We recommended that Management submit the above-noted lacking documents based on the checklist of requirements contained in the Municipality’s citizen’s charter for the Audit Team’s validation. Henceforth, we recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, examine/ ensure the completeness of documents before processing the payment/ liquidation relating to financial aid for medical and hospital expenses.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Engineer, do a more extensive site investigation during the preparation of detailed engineering plans.
We likewise recommended and Management, through the Municipal Planning and the Municipal Budget Officer, agreed to include in the proposed budget only those projects that are “procurement-and-implementation-ready” and defer those that still have unresolved issues until they become ready for immediate implementation.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to hasten the preparation of the BRS in order to validate the reconciling items on a timely basis.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:
Develop and maintain a GAD Database containing gender statistics and age- and sex-disaggregated data. This database should be regularly updated to serve as a basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation;
Support the GAD attributed programs with the result of the HGDG assessment and other necessary documents; and
Issue an Order creating a GAD M&E Team. This team shall be responsible for monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs and evaluating their outcomes to ensure the efficient and effective execution of gender-responsive initiatives.

We recommended that Management tighten their measures towards curbing/ cutting unnecessary expenditure by discontinuing the practice of incurring meals and snacks expenses during the conduct of the regular functions or duties of the personnel such as BAC meetings and conduct of project inspections, more so if the project involved is not a project of the Municipality.
The Municipal Accountant-Designate provide depreciation to the roofing repair with consideration on how much is the remaining useful life of the Municipal Slaughterhouse.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the schedules in support of the account balances as at December 31, 2019 and maintain subsidiary ledger/s for each control account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the subject reciprocal accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments to arrive at an equal and correct balances; and conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to adjust and correct any difference.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation on the depreciable assets and make the necessary adjusting entries to arrive at the correct net book value of the subject assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe proper recognition of asset accounts in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards and to take up adjusting entries by reversing previously made accounting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies using the perpetual inventory method by setting Inventory Accounts; and recognize expense only when inventories are distributed to the end-users with the required SSMI and RIS.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor review the disbursement process to address the reasons of the delay in the submission of DVs and other reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to correctly account the unexpended/unobligated DRRM Fund in the current year and preceding five years and provide a report to the Municipal Accountant for the orderly recording of such to the Trust Liability-DRRM account; and
instruct the Local DRRM Officer to update the Local DRRM Fund Investment Plan to include programs and projects to be funded under the unspent DRRM Fund.
We recommended that Management create an IAS Office to establish a sound internal control system that strengthens accountability, and ensures ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to consider assigning personnel in reconciling and recording special accounts for economic enterprise accounts as well as the 20 per cent Development Fund in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipality revisit and familiarize themselves on the existing guidelines on extension of contract time. Also, we recommended that the Municipality submit the required documents in support of the contract time extensions.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat prepare a consolidated Annual Procurement Plan using the prescribed form based on all the PPMPs indicating all the required information therein as enumerated in Section 7.3.4 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity/Municipal Mayor.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor send the BAC Chairperson, members, and secretariat including the members of the Technical Working Group to training/s on procurement to professionalize its procurement officials in order to promote and attain good governance by adhering to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and Submitted the Annual Procurement Plan.
observations.reason: Review of the APP for CY 2018 was in the prescribed format, however, information as to the mode of procurement particularly on alternative modes were not specified as to which is to be employed. instruct the Municipal Budget officer to correctly account the unexpended/unobligated DRRMF in the preceding five years and provide a report to the Municipal Accountant for the orderly recording to the Trust Liability – DRRM account; and

observe proper notification to the public pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 and the amendment under COA Circular No. 2016-003; and

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat thru the BAC to post all Notices of Award together with the Notices to Proceed and the approved contracts of their procurement opportunities and results thereof in the PhilGEPS and LGU websites  in compliance with the  the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat prepare a consolidated Annual Procurement Plan using the prescribed form based on all the PPMPs indicating all the required information therein as enumerated in Section 7.3.4 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity/Municipal Mayor.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor send the BAC Chairperson, members, and secretariat including the members of the Technical Working Group to training/s on procurement to professionalize its procurement officials in order to promote and attain good governance by adhering to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
We recommend that the current municipal Local School Board revisit the provisions of Section 98 of RA 7160 and reorganize the composition of the LSB.

We recommend that the municipal treasurer prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Prop. (IIRUP) on the LGUs unserviceable assets, furnish the municipal accountant with a copy of such report for purposes of reclassifi-cation, adjustment, or reconciliation of amounts.  We further recommend that the  municipal treasurer carry out the appropriate disposal of said unserviceable properties.

We recommend that, henceforth, management observe strictly Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-004 and Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting and liquidation of cash advances.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Accountant refrain from granting cash advance other than travel and intelligence fund to elected officials.
